title: AI agents accessing production database
contentSnippet:
So we have arrived at a point, our agent neeeds to interact with real production database.
So if you have implemented agents beyond the prototype stage, 
1. what does your architecture is like, 
2. any guardrails or runtime we need to custom build and
3. problems you have faced when it reached production.
So this is my first time implementing agents in production so rather than product recommendations please do give your learnings from real implementation.
Client: a smb(mostly internal work)

title: Ask HN: Why aren't we collaborating on the prompts we give to our AI agents?
contentSnippet:
Now that code is so cheap, I feel like the real work is the spec, the context, the plan (aka the prompt) you hand over to the AI agent. That's where you decide what's actually getting built and how.
That step still seems to be completely individual. You write it alone, the plan you formulate is local and private, and your coworkers only see the result when the PR is opened. On a team, that's the exact moment alignment should happen, and it's the one place we don't work together nowadays.
GitHub Next recently showed a prototype that gets close to this collaborative experience: https://maggieappleton.com/zero-alignment
I also wonder how other knowledge workers work and how they review each others' work. I believe they would also benefit from a "collaborative prompting" environment, where everyone sees what the others are working on and can step in at any point.
Why isn't this normal yet? Missing tooling, cultural habit, or does collaborating on a prompt not help at all?
Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48831425
Points: 1
# Comments: 0

title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
contentSnippet: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
contentSnippet: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
contentSnippet: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
contentSnippet: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
contentSnippet: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
contentSnippet: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
contentSnippet: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

title: Tether invests in Mercado Bitcoin as blockchain finance expands in Latin America
contentSnippet: Mercado Bitcoin will use the funding to expand tokenized finance across Latin America, adding to Tether's growing portfolio of infrastructure investments.

title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
contentSnippet: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
contentSnippet: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

title: Vitalik Buterin confirms AI identified his anonymous Ethereum proposal contribution
contentSnippet: The Ethereum co-founder said AI recognized his intellectual habits, ending a two-week public challenge to identify an anonymous contribution he made.

title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
contentSnippet: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
contentSnippet: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
contentSnippet: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
contentSnippet: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
contentSnippet: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
contentSnippet: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
contentSnippet: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.

title: TeraWulf shares rise after $19B Anthropic AI lease, JV sale
contentSnippet: The Bitcoin miner signed a 20-year AI infrastructure lease with Anthropic and sold its majority stake in a separate AI data center joint venture.

title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
contentSnippet: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
contentSnippet: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.

title: FCA warns of major shakeup as AI agents meet tokenized money
contentSnippet: The FCA’s vision for agentic AI points toward a financial system where programmable money and tokenized assets could play a much larger role.

title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
contentSnippet: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.

title: Central bankers sound alarms over agentic AI finance risks
contentSnippet: “We need to think about new tools and a different way of working with the [AI] market in a more collaborative way,” says Nikhil Rathi, CEO of the UK’s finance watchdog.

title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
contentSnippet: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.

title: Moonbeam to pivot from Polkadot to Base, unveils AI agent framework
contentSnippet: Moonbeam didn’t provide a timeline for when it would launch the AI agent platform, but told GLMR holders to bridge their tokens from the Polkadot parachain to Base before July 31.

title: AI agent development hasn’t accelerated as expected, Zuckerberg says
contentSnippet: Mark Zuckerberg’s comments came on the same day that Meta expanded its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp.

title: OpenAI weighs 5% stake for US government amid Trump talks: FT
contentSnippet: OpenAI has reportedly discussed giving the US government a 5% equity stake in early Trump administration talks as Washington tightens AI model oversight.

title: Venice AI becomes unicorn after $65M Series A as AI privacy concerns grow
contentSnippet: The funding round is Venice AI’s first external capital raise since it launched in May 2024.

title: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
contentSnippet: Claude Fable 5 will be redeployed with a new set of classifiers designed to identify and block a broader range of cybersecurity-related tasks.
